  "findings": [
    "No thermal expansion tank installed on a closed plumbing system",
    "Excessive system pressure risk due to thermal expansion during heating cycles"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an approved thermal expansion tank on the cold water supply line to the water heater",
    "Verify the operation and rating of the temperature and pressure (T&P) relief valve"
  ],
  "rationale": "A closed plumbing system (e.g., one with a backflow preventer, check valve, or pressure-reducing valve) prevents expanded water from flowing back into the public main. Without an expansion tank, heating water causes rapid pressure spikes that can damage the water heater, piping, and fixtures, or cause the T&P valve to discharge repeatedly.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 504",
    "IRC P2804"
  ]
}
